arts.ttl: Napoli Taking Steps to Beat Chelsea to Man Utd Outcast
arts.sum: Napoli and Manchester United re-established contact for Argentine forward Alejandro Garnacho on Friday. According to Gianluca Di Marzio, the Serie A pacesetters renewed talks with the Red Devils yesterday as they bid to complete a mouth-watering double swoop this winter. The post Napoli Taking Steps to Beat Chelsea to Man Utd Outcast appeared first on The Cult of Calcio.

arts.ttl: Maresca makes Garnacho transfer U-turn but £60m Chelsea deal still on
arts.sum: football.london understands the Blues are preparing an offer for Garnacho, who is said to be valued at around £60million by Manchester United, as the west Londoners step up their interest in the 20-year-old wide man. Chelsea are exploring their options in the left wing department in the January transfer window and with 10 days remaining until the February 3 deadline, Garnacho seems to be the main target in that position despite interest in Jamie Gittens and Mathys Tel as well.

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca and Chelsea must learn from PSG and exploit Manchester City’s two clear weaknesses - opinion
arts.sum: Chelsea’s trip to Manchester City this weekend is usually one that the supporters would be absolutely dreading, but there’s definitely more reason to be optimistic...

arts.ttl: Man Utd REIGNITE pursuit of €70m Chelsea star
arts.sum: However, Tel has so far shown no indication that he wants to leave Munich and this is making a deal difficult, especially as Chelsea are demanding a whopping €70 million for Nkunku. Manchester United have reignited their interest in Chelsea striker Christopher Nkunku, according to reliable reports.

arts.sum: Pep Guardiola had a number of things to get off his chest ahead of Manchester City's Premier League visit of Chelsea.

arts.ttl: Chelsea 'set' Christopher Nkunku transfer price as Potter rejects chance to help
arts.sum: Firstly, that Chelsea do not want to sell Nkunku and secondly, they sense that United are desperate to get a deal done. Ahead of another busy weekend, football.london has taken a closer look at some of the current headlines circulating around the club.
